volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: The Two Menaechmuses (Section 5.9.1069 - Section 5.9.1121b)

sections.label: Section 5.9.1069
sections.translation: I am a Sicilian , of Syracuse .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1069b
sections.original: Eadem urbs et patria est mihi .
sections.translation: Troth , the same is my native country .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1070
sections.translation: What is it that I hear of you ?

sections.label: Section 5.9.1070b
sections.translation: That which is the fact .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1070c
sections.original: Novi equidem hunc : erus est meus .
sections.translation: I know this person myself ( pointing to the other MENAECHMUS ) ; he is my master , I really am his servant ; but I did think I belonged to this other . ( To MENAECHMUS of Epidamnus , by mistake .) I took him to be you ; to him , too , did I give some trouble . ( To his master .) Pray , pardon me if I have said aught foolishly or unadvisedly to you .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1074
sections.original: Delirare mihi videre : non commeministi , simul
sections.translation: You seem to me to be mad . Don ’ t you remember that together with me you disembarked from board ship to-day ?

sections.label: Section 5.9.1075b
sections.translation: Why , really , you say what ’ s right — you are my master ; ( to MENAECHMUS of Epidamnus ) do you look out for a servant . ( To his master .) To you my greetings ( to MENAECHMUS of Epidamnus ) to you , farewell . This , I say , is Menaechmus .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1077b
sections.translation: But I say I am .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1077c
sections.translation: What story ’ s this ? Are you Menaechmus ?

sections.label: Section 5.9.1078b
sections.original: Me esse dico , Moscho prognatum patre .
sections.translation: I say that I ’ m the son of Moschus , who was my father .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1079
sections.translation: Are you the son of my father ?

sections.label: Section 5.9.1079b
sections.translation: Aye , I really am , young man , of my own father .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1080
sections.original: tuom tibi neque occupare neque praeripere postulo .
sections.translation: I don ’ t want to claim your father , nor to take possession of him from you .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1081
sections.original: Di immortales , spem insperatam date mihi quam suspicor .
sections.translation: Immortal Gods , what unhoped-for hope do you bestow on me , as I suspect . For unless my mind misleads me , these are the two twin-brothers ; for they mention alike their native country and their father . I ’ ll call my master aside — Menaechmus .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1084b
sections.translation: What do you want ?

sections.label: Section 5.9.1084c
sections.translation: I don ’ t want you both .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1085
sections.original: sed uter vostrorum est advectus mecum navi .
sections.translation: But which of you was brought here in the ship with me ?

sections.label: Section 5.9.1085b
sections.translation: Not I .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1086
sections.translation: But ’ twas I .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1086b
sections.translation: You , then , I want . Step this way .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1086c
sections.translation: I ’ ve stepped aside now . What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 5.9.1087
sections.original: Illic homó aut sycophanta aut geminus est frater tuos .
sections.translation: This man is either an impostor , or he is your twin-brother . But I never beheld one person more like another person . Neither water , believe me , is ever more like to water nor milk to milk ,

sections.label: Section 5.9.1090
sections.original: quam hic tui est , tuque huius autem ; post eandem patriam ac patrem
sections.translation: than he is to you , and you likewise to him ; besides , he speaks of the same native country and father . ’ Tis better for us to accost him and make further enquiries of him .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1092
sections.original: Hercle qui tu me admonuisti recte , et habeo gratiam .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , but you ’ ve given me good advice , and I return you thanks . Troth , now , prithee , do continue to lend me your assistance . If you discover that this is my brother , be you a free man .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1094b
sections.translation: I hope I shall .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1094c
sections.translation: I too hope that it will be so .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1095
sections.original: Quid ais tu ? Menaechmum , opinor , te vocari dixeras .
sections.translation: How say you ? I think you said that you are called Menaechmus ?

sections.label: Section 5.9.1096
sections.translation: I did so indeed .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1096b
sections.original: Huic item Menaechmo nomen est . in Sicilia
sections.translation: His name , too , is Menaechmus . You said that you were born at Syracuse , in Sicily ; he was born there . You said that Moschus was your father ; he was his as well . Now both of you can be giving help to me and to yourselves at the same time .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1100
sections.original: Promeruisti ut ne quid ores quod velis , quin impetres .
sections.translation: You have deserved that you should beg nothing but what you should obtain that which you desire . Free as I am , I ’ ll serve you as though you . had bought me for money .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1102
sections.original: Spes mihi est , vos inventurum fratres germanos duos
sections.translation: I have a hope that I shall find that you two are twin-born brothers , born of one mother and of one father on the same day .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1104
sections.original: Mira memoras . utinam efficere quod pollicitu'spollicitus es possies .
sections.translation: You mention wondrous things . I wish that you could effect what you ’ ve promised .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1105
sections.original: Possum . sed nunc agite uterque id quod rogabo dicite .
sections.translation: I can . But attend now , both of you , and tell me that which I shall ask .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1106
sections.original: Vbi lubet , roga : réspondebo . nil reticebo quod sciam .
sections.translation: Ask as you please , I ’ ll answer you . I ’ ll not conceal anything that I know .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1107
sections.translation: Isn ’ t your name Menaechmus ?

sections.label: Section 5.9.1107b
sections.translation: I own it .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1107c
sections.translation: Isn ’ t it yours as well ?

sections.label: Section 5.9.1108
sections.translation: It is .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1108b
sections.translation: Do you say that Moschus was your father ?

sections.label: Section 5.9.1108c
sections.translation: Truly , I do say so .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1108d
sections.translation: And mine as well .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1109
sections.translation: Are you of Syracuse ?

sections.label: Section 5.9.1109b
sections.translation: Certainly .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1109c
sections.translation: And you ?

sections.label: Section 5.9.1109d
sections.translation: Why not the same ?

sections.label: Section 5.9.1110
sections.original: Optime usque adhuc conveniunt signa . porro operam date .
sections.translation: Hitherto the marks agree perfectly well . Still lend me your attention . ( To MENAECHMUS .) Tell me , what do you remember at the greatest distance of time in your native country ?

sections.label: Section 5.9.1112
sections.original: Cum patre ut abii Tarentum ád mercatum , postea
sections.translation: When I went with my father to Tarentum to traffic ; and afterwards how I strayed away from my father among the people , and was carried away thence .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1114
sections.translation: Supreme Jupiter , preserve me !

sections.label: Section 5.9.1114b
sections.translation: Why do you exclaim ? Why don ’ t you hold your peace ?

sections.label: Section 5.9.1115
sections.original: quot eras annos gnatus , quom te pater a patria ávehit ?
sections.translation: ( To MENAECHMUS .) How many years old were you when your father took you from your native country ?

sections.label: Section 5.9.1116
sections.original: Septuennis : nam tunc dentes mihi cadebant primulum .
sections.translation: Seven years old ; for just then my teeth were changing for the first time . And never since then have I seen my father .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1117b
sections.translation: Well , how many sons of you had your father then ?

sections.label: Section 5.9.1118b
sections.translation: As far as I now remember , two .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1119
sections.original: Vter eratis , tun an ille , maior ?
sections.translation: Which of the two was the older — you or the other ?

sections.label: Section 5.9.1119b
sections.translation: Both were just alike in age .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1120
sections.translation: How can that be ?

sections.label: Section 5.9.1120b
sections.translation: We two were twins .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1120c
sections.translation: The Gods wish to bless me .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1121
sections.translation: If you interrupt , I shall hold my tongue .

sections.label: Section 5.9.1121b
sections.translation: Rather than that , I ’ ll hold my tongue .
